prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
# Done

The last period lands like a stone
dropped into still water —
all that motion spreading out,
then quiet.

My hands don't know what to do.
They hover, half-reaching
for the weight that was there
every morning for months.

Strange, how the absence
has its own kind of gravity.
Strange, how I miss the wanting
almost as much as the work.

But underneath the hollow
something is settling, slow and warm —
the knowledge that I carried it,
that I saw the whole long thing through.
